extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR won Stage 17 to Peyragudes with multiple late attacks, gaining only 4 seconds on VINGEGAARD despite UAE's all-day effort with McNulty pacing.
tactical_patterns: McNulty's extended pacing through final 30km failed to crack Vingegaard despite UAE numerical disadvantage with only three domestics.
Pogacar's repeated attacks on penultimate climb summit and final 5km gained zero time, signaling defensive stalemate tactics.
Jumbo-Visma isolated Vingegaard early but his lone defense matched UAE's coordinated assault, revealing superform sustainability.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: absorbed multiple Pogacar attacks while isolated, maintaining GC gap at 2:18 without team support
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: rode independently on final climb to secure podium buffer at 4:56, increasing gap to Quintana
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: executed extended domestique shift through 30km despite UAE being down to three riders
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: dropped early on first Pyrenean stage, exposing Ineos weakness before final climbs
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: negative: dropped by 30km mark on major mountain stage, revealing grand tour climbing limitations
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: secured first-ever top-10 position, moving into fifth overall through consistent climbing
